sections.label: Business Outcomes
sections.description: The top-level KPIs a client's CFO cares about: money earned (Revenue) and money saved (Gross Margin), rolling up into Net Profit and Customer Lifetime Value.

sections.label: Traffic & Acquisition
sections.description: How visitors arrive and what it costs to bring them. Sessions by channel, conversion rate, bounce, and customer acquisition cost.

sections.label: Product & Browsing
sections.description: The browsing experience that turns sessions into intent: product views, add-to-cart, page speed, imagery, reviews, and search.

sections.label: Cart & Basket Value
sections.description: What ends up in the basket and how big it is: cart progression, items per order, item price, cross-sell, bundling, and discounting.

sections.label: Checkout
sections.description: The final, highest-leverage conversion step: checkout completion and the friction points that drive abandonment.

sections.label: Fulfillment & Returns
sections.description: Getting the order to the customer and handling what comes back: shipping cost, fulfillment cost, delivery speed, and returns.

sections.label: Retention & Loyalty
sections.description: Turning one purchase into many: repeat purchase rate, purchase frequency, lifespan, email engagement, and loyalty.

sections.label: Cost & Margin
sections.description: The cost lines that erode margin: cost of goods, payment processing fees, and cost to serve. Lowering these is money saved.

nodes.name: Net Profit
nodes.definition: The money a webshop ultimately keeps: everything earned minus everything spent. The apex both the money-earned and money-saved branches ladder up into.
nodes.formula: Revenue - Total Cost

levers.action: Grow revenue without growing cost in step
levers.description: Lift conversion and AOV faster than variable cost rises.
Reduce returns, fees and discount leakage.

nodes.name: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
nodes.definition: Total margin a customer is expected to generate across their whole relationship with the shop. Connects retention to long-run profit.
nodes.formula: Average Order Value x Purchase Frequency x Customer Lifespan

parents.note: Higher lifetime value per customer lifts long-run profit.

benchmarks.label: Repeat spend
benchmarks.value: Existing customers spend ~67% more than new ones

nodes.name: Gross Margin
nodes.definition: Share of revenue left after the variable cost of selling (goods, shipping, fees, returns). The headline money-saved KPI: lifting it is money saved.
nodes.formula: (Revenue - Variable Costs) / Revenue x 100

parents.note: Higher margin per order flows straight to profit.

nodes.name: Revenue
nodes.definition: Total money earned from orders in a period. The headline money-earned KPI.
nodes.formula: Orders x Average Order Value

parents.note: Revenue is the money-earned half of profit.

nodes.name: Total Cost
nodes.definition: All money spent to earn revenue: goods, fulfillment, shipping, fees, returns, acquisition and service. Lowering it is money saved.
nodes.formula: COGS + Fulfillment + Shipping + Fees + Returns + Acquisition + Cost to Serve

parents.note: Total spend; reducing it is money saved.

levers.action: Attack the largest cost lines first

nodes.name: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
nodes.definition: Average marketing and sales spend to win one new customer. A cost line; lowering it is money saved.
nodes.formula: Marketing & Sales Spend / New Customers Acquired

parents.note: Acquisition spend is an operating cost.

levers.description: More orders per click lowers effective CAC.

nodes.name: Orders
nodes.definition: Number of completed purchases in a period.
nodes.formula: Sessions x Conversion Rate

parents.note: One of the two factors of revenue.

nodes.name: Average Order Value (AOV)
nodes.definition: Average revenue per order. The basket-size half of revenue and a factor of lifetime value.
nodes.formula: Revenue / Orders  (= Items per Order x Average Item Price)

parents.note: The basket-size factor of revenue.
One factor of lifetime value.

levers.action: Set a free-shipping threshold above AOV

nodes.name: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
nodes.definition: Direct landed cost of the products sold. The primary driver of gross margin.
nodes.formula: Units Sold x Landed Unit Cost

parents.note: The largest variable cost line for most shops.
COGS is the primary driver of gross margin.

nodes.name: Cost to Serve
nodes.definition: Cost of customer support and service per order. Often driven by avoidable delivery and returns issues.
nodes.formula: Support + Service Cost / Orders

parents.note: Service and support is an operating cost line.

nodes.name: Payment Processing Fees
nodes.definition: Fees paid on each transaction to processors and networks. A small percentage that compounds across every order.
nodes.formula: Transactions x (Interchange + Assessment + Gateway markup)

parents.note: A per-transaction cost line.
Fees eat contribution margin on every order.

nodes.name: Fulfillment Cost per Order
nodes.definition: Cost to pick, pack and handle an order in the warehouse. A controllable margin cost.
nodes.formula: Pick + Pack + Warehouse + Handling Cost / Orders

parents.note: Fulfillment is a variable cost line.
Fulfillment spend eats contribution margin.

nodes.name: Returns Cost
nodes.definition: Total cost of handling returns: reverse shipping, inspection, restocking and write-offs.
nodes.formula: Reverse Logistics + Restocking + Write-offs per period

parents.note: Returns handling is a variable cost line.
Returns cost eats contribution margin.

levers.action: Lower the return rate at source

nodes.name: Shipping Cost
nodes.definition: Outbound shipping spend per order. A major margin cost line and the most-absorbed cost in e-commerce.
nodes.formula: Outbound Shipping Spend / Orders (or % of revenue)

parents.note: Shipping is a variable cost line.
Shipping spend eats contribution margin.

nodes.name: Customer Lifespan
nodes.definition: Average duration a customer keeps buying. A multiplicative factor of lifetime value.
nodes.formula: Average active relationship duration (years)

parents.note: Lifespan factor of CLV.

nodes.name: Purchase Frequency
nodes.definition: How often a customer orders in a period. A multiplicative factor of lifetime value.
nodes.formula: Orders / Unique Customers per period

parents.note: Frequency factor of CLV.

nodes.name: Conversion Rate
nodes.definition: Share of sessions that result in an order. The single most-watched funnel KPI, and the product of every funnel stage.
nodes.formula: Product-View Rate x Add-to-Cart Rate x Cart-to-Checkout Rate x Checkout-Completion Rate

parents.note: The other factor of orders.
A higher conversion rate spreads acquisition spend over more orders, lowering CAC.

levers.action: Fix the weakest funnel stage first

nodes.name: Sessions / Traffic
nodes.definition: Visits to the shop in a period, the top of the funnel.
nodes.formula: Organic + Paid + Email + Social sessions

parents.note: Traffic is one factor of orders.

nodes.name: Average Item Price
nodes.definition: Average realized price per unit sold. The price factor of AOV.
nodes.formula: Revenue / Total Units Sold

parents.note: Price factor of AOV.

nodes.name: Items per Order
nodes.definition: Average number of units in an order. One of the two AOV factors.
nodes.formula: Total Units Sold / Orders

parents.note: Units factor of AOV.

nodes.name: Support Contact Rate
nodes.definition: Support contacts per order. Each contact carries a cost; many are avoidable.
nodes.formula: Support Contacts / Orders x 100

parents.note: Each contact adds to cost to serve.

levers.description: Proactive tracking removes the top contact reason.

nodes.name: Landed Unit Cost
nodes.definition: Fully loaded cost to get one unit into the warehouse, ready to sell.
nodes.formula: Supplier Price + Inbound Freight + Duties per unit

parents.note: Per-unit landed cost drives total COGS.

nodes.name: Carrier Rate
nodes.definition: Negotiated blended cost per parcel by carrier, zone and weight. Sets the base of shipping cost.
nodes.formula: Blended Cost per Parcel (carrier/zone/weight)

parents.note: Negotiated carrier rates set the base shipping cost.

levers.action: Use regional carriers for some zones

nodes.name: Packaging Cost
nodes.definition: Materials cost per shipped order.
nodes.formula: Packaging Materials Cost / Order

parents.note: Packaging is part of per-order fulfillment cost.

nodes.name: Return Rate
nodes.definition: Share of orders returned. A cross-branch KPI: returns reduce net revenue and drive reverse-logistics cost.
nodes.formula: Returned Orders / Orders x 100

parents.note: Returns volume drives reverse-logistics cost.
Returned orders reduce net revenue.

nodes.name: Reverse Logistics Cost per Return
nodes.definition: Cost to process one return: return shipping, inspection and restocking.
nodes.formula: Return Shipping + Inspection + Restock Cost / Return

parents.note: Per-return processing cost.

nodes.name: Warehouse Efficiency
nodes.definition: Productivity of pick, pack and ship operations. Drives per-order handling cost.
nodes.formula: Orders Fulfilled / Labor Hour (or cost per pick)

parents.note: Pick/pack productivity sets handling cost.

levers.action: Add automation where volume justifies it

nodes.name: Repeat Purchase Rate
nodes.definition: Share of customers who buy more than once. The core retention KPI; small lifts compound into lifetime value.
nodes.formula: Customers with >=2 Orders / Total Customers x 100

parents.note: Returning customers generate additional orders.
More repeat purchases extend lifetime value.

benchmarks.label: Compounding effect
benchmarks.value: 27% buy again after the 1st order; 54% after the 3rd

nodes.name: Bounce Rate
nodes.definition: Share of sessions that leave without interacting. Bounced sessions never enter the funnel, so a high bounce rate caps conversion.
nodes.formula: Single-page Sessions / Total Sessions x 100

parents.note: Higher bounce means fewer sessions reach the funnel.

levers.action: Match landing pages to ad intent

nodes.name: Email / CRM Sessions
nodes.definition: Sessions from owned email and CRM flows. The lowest-cost, highest-converting channel.
nodes.formula: List Size x Send Frequency x Click-Through Rate

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

nodes.name: Organic Sessions
nodes.definition: Sessions from unpaid search and content.
nodes.formula: Sessions attributed to organic search / content

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

nodes.name: Paid Sessions
nodes.definition: Sessions from paid media (search, social, display).
nodes.formula: Ad Spend / Cost per Click

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

levers.action: Lower cost per click via quality score

nodes.name: Social Sessions
nodes.definition: Sessions from organic and paid social.
nodes.formula: Sessions attributed to social platforms

parents.note: One traffic channel summing into total sessions.

nodes.name: Cart-to-Checkout Rate
nodes.definition: Share of carts that proceed to start checkout.
nodes.formula: Checkouts Started / Carts Created x 100

parents.note: Third multiplicative stage of the conversion funnel.

levers.action: Show all-in price in the cart

nodes.name: Cross-Sell Attach Rate
nodes.definition: Share of orders that include a cross-sold (complementary) item. A direct lever on items per order.
nodes.formula: Orders with >=1 Cross-sold Item / Orders x 100

parents.note: Cross-sell adds units to the basket.

levers.action: Cross-sell on the cart and PDP

nodes.name: Discount Depth
nodes.definition: Average size of discounts given. Discounts cut realized price and come straight off margin. A cross-branch margin eroder.
nodes.formula: Discount Value / Gross Order Value x 100

parents.note: Deeper discounts cut realized item price.
Every discount point comes straight off margin.

levers.action: Replace blanket codes with targeted offers
Prefer free shipping over price cuts

nodes.name: Free-Shipping Threshold
nodes.definition: The basket value above which shipping is free. Set above AOV, it nudges shoppers to add items, but absorbs shipping cost on qualifying orders.
nodes.formula: Threshold set ~15-30% above current AOV

parents.note: A threshold above AOV nudges shoppers to add items.
Absorbed shipping on qualifying orders is a cost.

benchmarks.label: AOV uplift
benchmarks.value: ~12-24% higher AOV; ~58% add an item to qualify

nodes.name: Product Bundling
nodes.definition: Selling curated multi-product bundles, raising units and value per order.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: bundle availability and uptake

parents.note: Bundles add units per order.

levers.action: Create value bundles around hero SKUs

nodes.name: Upsell Rate
nodes.definition: Share of orders where the customer accepts a higher-value variant or tier.
nodes.formula: Orders with Upsell Accepted / Orders x 100

parents.note: Upselling to higher-value variants raises item price.

nodes.name: Checkout Completion Rate
nodes.definition: Share of started checkouts that result in a completed order. The final, highest-leverage funnel stage.
nodes.formula: Completed Checkouts / Started Checkouts x 100

parents.note: Final multiplicative stage of the conversion funnel.

benchmarks.label: Checkout usability upside (Baymard)
benchmarks.value: Up to +35% conversion from fixing checkout UX

nodes.name: Self-Service Resolution Rate
nodes.definition: Share of customer issues resolved without an agent. Deflects contacts and lowers cost to serve.
nodes.formula: Issues Resolved via Self-service / Total Issues x 100

parents.note: Higher self-service lowers cost to serve.
Self-service deflects agent contacts.

levers.action: Build a help center and order self-service

nodes.name: Add-to-Cart Rate
nodes.definition: Share of sessions that add a product to the cart. Signals product-page persuasiveness and intent.
nodes.formula: Sessions with Add-to-Cart / Sessions x 100

parents.note: Second multiplicative stage of the conversion funnel.

levers.action: Add clear pricing and stock signals

nodes.name: Product-View Rate
nodes.definition: Share of sessions that view at least one product. The first funnel stage after landing.
nodes.formula: Product-Viewing Sessions / Sessions x 100

parents.note: First multiplicative stage of the conversion funnel.

nodes.name: Loyalty Program
nodes.definition: Rewards, points or tiers that incentivize repeat purchase.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: enrolled share and reward structure

parents.note: Rewards increase repeat rate.

nodes.name: Post-Purchase Experience
nodes.definition: The quality of everything after checkout: tracking, packaging, onboarding and support. Builds loyalty and reduces returns.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: tracking, unboxing, onboarding, support quality

parents.note: A smooth post-purchase experience builds loyalty.
Good onboarding and care reduce returns.

nodes.name: Cart Transparency
nodes.definition: How clearly the cart shows the full cost (incl. shipping and tax) before checkout. Hidden costs are the top abandonment cause.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: total cost visible before checkout

parents.note: Showing the full price in-cart prevents later drop-off.

levers.action: Show shipping and tax in the cart

nodes.name: Product Recommendations
nodes.definition: Algorithmic suggestions that surface relevant products. Deepen browsing and drive cross-sell.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: relevance and placement of recommendations

parents.note: Recommendation engines surface relevant add-ons.
Recommendations deepen browsing.

nodes.name: Checkout Abandonment Rate
nodes.definition: Share of started checkouts that are abandoned before completion. The inverse of completion, and a rich source of recoverable revenue.
nodes.formula: 1 - Checkout Completion Rate (Abandoned / Started x 100)

parents.note: Completion = 1 - abandonment.

levers.action: Shorten the form and add wallets

nodes.name: Price Clarity
nodes.definition: How early and completely the all-in price (incl. shipping and tax) is visible. Surprises later cause drop-off.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: all-in price visible early, no late surprises

parents.note: Clear pricing builds confidence to add to cart.
No price surprises in the cart keeps shoppers moving.

levers.action: Show shipping and tax estimates early

nodes.name: Product Detail Page Quality
nodes.definition: How well a product page answers buying questions and builds confidence. A composite driver of add-to-cart.
nodes.formula: Qualitative index of imagery, reviews, price clarity and stock signals

parents.note: Better PDPs lift add-to-cart.

nodes.name: On-Site Search Quality
nodes.definition: How well internal search returns relevant results. Searchers convert at much higher rates than browsers.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: relevance, zero-result rate, synonym handling

parents.note: Good search gets shoppers to products faster.

nodes.name: Stock Availability
nodes.definition: Share of catalogue that is in stock and buyable. Out-of-stock items cannot convert.
nodes.formula: In-Stock SKUs / Total SKUs x 100

parents.note: Out-of-stock items cannot be added to cart.

nodes.name: Email / CRM Engagement
nodes.definition: How actively customers open, click and convert on owned email and CRM flows. Brings customers back at near-zero marginal cost.
nodes.formula: Open / Click / Conversion rates of CRM flows

parents.note: Owned-channel engagement brings customers back.
Engagement drives email-sourced sessions.

nodes.name: Checkout Form Length
nodes.definition: Number of fields a shopper must complete to check out. Each extra field adds friction.
nodes.formula: Count of required checkout form fields

parents.note: Longer forms raise abandonment.

benchmarks.label: Checkout length (Baymard)
benchmarks.value: Reducing form fields measurably lifts completion

nodes.name: Mobile Checkout Optimization
nodes.definition: How well checkout works on mobile, where most traffic is and conversion lags desktop.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: autofill, wallets, responsive layout, tap targets

parents.note: Mobile converts worse; optimizing it recovers orders.

levers.action: Enable wallets and autofill on mobile
Simplify mobile layout and tap targets

nodes.name: Payment Method Coverage
nodes.definition: How well the offered payment methods match local shopper preferences. A missing preferred method causes drop-off.
nodes.formula: Count of locally relevant payment methods offered

parents.note: Missing a preferred payment method causes abandonment.

levers.action: Add local methods (e.g. iDEAL, SEPA, BNPL)

nodes.name: Trust & Security Signals
nodes.definition: Visible cues that the shop and payment are safe (SSL, recognizable marks, guarantees, reviews). Distrust drives a quarter of abandonment.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: SSL, trust badges, guarantees, recognizable payment marks

parents.note: Payment-security doubts cause ~25% of abandonment.
Trust cues raise willingness to add to cart.

levers.action: Show recognizable trust and payment marks

nodes.name: Unexpected Extra Costs
nodes.definition: Surprise shipping, tax or fees revealed late in checkout. The single biggest abandonment driver.
nodes.formula: Share of abandoners citing extra costs

parents.note: The #1 abandonment reason (48%).

nodes.name: Delivery Speed
nodes.definition: Time from order to doorstep. Slow promised delivery drives abandonment; fast, reliable delivery drives loyalty.
nodes.formula: Median Order-to-Doorstep time (days)

parents.note: Slow promised delivery drives ~23% of abandonment.
Fast, reliable delivery drives repeat purchases.

benchmarks.label: Slow-delivery abandonment (Baymard)
benchmarks.value: ~23% abandon when delivery is too slow

nodes.name: Page Load Time / Site Speed
nodes.definition: How fast pages render and become interactive. Speed influences every funnel stage from bounce to checkout.
nodes.formula: Median Largest Contentful Paint (seconds)

parents.note: Slower pages reduce product views.
Slower pages reduce add-to-cart.
Slow pages raise checkout abandonment.
Slow first paint raises bounce.

nodes.name: Product Imagery
nodes.definition: Quality and richness of product visuals (zoom, video, 360, lifestyle). Drives both intent and accurate expectations.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: image count, zoom, video, 360 coverage

parents.note: Imagery is a core PDP quality factor.
Accurate, rich imagery sets expectations and reduces returns.

nodes.name: Reviews & Ratings
nodes.definition: Volume and average rating of customer reviews. Social proof that lifts conversion and calibrates expectations.
nodes.formula: Average Star Rating weighted by Review Volume

parents.note: Reviews are a core PDP quality factor.
Reviews calibrate expectations, lowering returns.

nodes.name: Image Optimization
nodes.definition: How well product and page images are compressed and delivered. Images are usually the largest part of page weight.
nodes.formula: Qualitative: next-gen formats, compression, lazy-load, correct sizing

parents.note: Image payload is typically the largest part of page weight.

nodes.name: Server Response Time (TTFB)
nodes.definition: Time for the server to return the first byte. A major, often-overlooked component of total load time.
nodes.formula: Time to First Byte (milliseconds)

parents.note: TTFB is a major component of load time.
